name: Maristan
theoryOfChange: Maristan describes an approach grounded in Islamic traditions that integrates professional clinical care, education, and research to advance holistic mental and spiritual wellness; it aims to bridge research with practice (as community partner of the Stanford Muslim Mental Health & Islamic Psychology Lab) and to equip religious, community, and clinical leaders through evidence-based training to address mental health crises within Muslim communities.
scoreSummary: Too early to rate numerically. This organization was founded in 2022 and is shown as an emerging organization while it builds a public track record.

evaluationSources: UC San Diego Center for Community Health
Stanford University (Muslim Mental Health & Islamic Psychology Lab)
Community Partner (Stanford University Muslim Mental Health & Islamic Psychology Lab)

zakatClaimEvidence: Help heal minds and hearts of our Ummah by supporting Maristan's essential, faith-centered mental health programs: Zakat-Eligible Mental Health Clinic, Healing & Learning Circle, Mental Health Trainings & Psychoeducation using evidence-based information to build mental health literacy and reduce stigma. 100% of your Zakat will go directly to Zakat-eligible clients receiving clinical therapy. (Source: https://amwkgvxs.donorsupport.co/page/FUNBFXDFWXS)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Early-stage organization. We show qualitative context while it builds a longer public track record.

The charity is zakat-eligible and maintains a zakat policy.

Maristan operates programs including the Maristan Clinic and Education and Training.

dimension_explanations.alignment: Maristan shows high donor fit for those seeking to support global health and mental health within a Muslim context, as it maintains a clear zakat policy [2]. The organization demonstrates strong fundraising efficiency, spending only $0.11 per $1 raised [1].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization focuses on capacity building through its Maristan Clinic and professional education programs [4][5]. While it maintains a healthy $658,402 in total revenue and 13.3 months of working capital, specific cost-per-beneficiary data is currently unavailable [1][3].

baseline_narrative.headline: Maristan is a community mental health organization dedicated to providing culturally sensitive clinical care and professional education.
strengths: Strong financial stability with 13.3 months of working capital
High fundraising efficiency with only $0.11 spent per dollar raised

alignment.explanation: Maristan is highly aligned with Muslim donor needs, offering <cite id="2">verified zakat eligibility</cite> for mental health services, a traditionally <cite id="2">underserved area in Islamic philanthropy</cite>.

impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that mental health interventions are both clinically effective and culturally safe.

strengths.detail: The organization maintains <cite id="1">13.3 months of working capital</cite>, providing a strong safety net for its clinical programs.
Maristan spends only <cite id="1">$0.11 to raise every dollar</cite>, significantly outperforming many peer organizations in the global health sector.
The charity achieved a <cite id="3">376.0% revenue CAGR</cite>, growing from approximately $130,000 to over $618,000 in a single year.

strengths_deep_dive: Maristan's primary strength lies in its specialized focus on Islamic-integrated mental health. By offering <cite id="4">Healing Circles and Suicide Response Certification</cite>, they address sensitive issues often stigmatized in community settings. Their financial model is currently very stable, with <cite id="3">$511,126 in net assets</cite> supporting a relatively lean operation. This stability is bolstered by a diverse revenue stream that includes <cite id="3">$147,687 in program service revenue</cite>, indicating that their clinical and educational services generate earned income alongside traditional <cite id="3">contributions of $470,945</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: Maristan is a specialized community mental health organization that bridges the gap between Islamic tradition and modern psychology. The organization provides <cite id="4">culturally sensitive clinical care</cite> and professional education to address the unique mental health needs of Muslim communities. Maristan reported <cite id="3">total revenue of $618,632</cite> in 2023, representing a significant <cite id="3">376.0% revenue growth</cite> from the previous year. This rapid expansion reflects a high demand for its specialized services, which include the <cite id="4">Maristan Clinic, research initiatives, and healing circles</cite>. The charity operates with high efficiency in its resource mobilization, maintaining <cite id="1">fundraising efficiency of $0.11 per $1 raised</cite>. This lean approach to development ensures that a vast majority of donor contributions are available for its core mission of <cite id="5">community mental health and capacity building</cite>. As an emerging institution, Maristan focuses on <cite id="4">suicide response certification training</cite> and clinical education to empower other providers. The organization is <cite id="2">verified as zakat-eligible</cite>, specifically serving the asnaf categories of <cite id="2">fuqara (the poor), masakin (the needy), and fisabilillah</cite>.
